texto: Andrew Ross makes sense of this sad artifice [decreasing academic pay] by explaining that academics of all ranks, along with artists, are uniquely willing to tolerate exploitation in the workplace. Ross claims that scholars' readiness "to accept a discounted wage out of 'love for their subject' has helped not only to sustain the cheap labor supply but also to magnify its strength and volume. Like artists and performers, academics are inclined by training to sacrifice earnings for the opportunity to exercise their craft." (p. 64)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Frank Donoghue: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Andrew Ross makes sense of this sad artifice [decreasing academic pay] by explaining that academi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, education, education-salaries pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e trabalho.
